sections.heading: What is a Ballistic Coefficient Calculator?
sections.content: A Ballistic Coefficient (BC) Calculator is a free online tool that measures a projectile’s aerodynamic efficiency. It calculates how well a projectile overcomes air resistance using its mass, drag coefficient, and cross-sectional area.

sections.heading: How to Use the Calculator
sections.content: Follow these simple steps to calculate the ballistic coefficient:

subSections.heading: Steps
subSections.content:
- Open the Calculator – Visit the BC Calculator page in your browser.
- Enter Mass (m) – Input the mass of the projectile in kg or g.
- Enter Drag Coefficient (Cd) – Provide the projectile’s drag coefficient.
- Enter Cross-Sectional Area (A) – Input the frontal area in m².
- Click Calculate – The calculator instantly shows the ballistic coefficient.
- Tip: Keep units consistent for accurate results.

subSections.heading: Features
subSections.content:
- Instant Calculation – Get results in seconds.
- Simple & Mobile-Friendly – Works on desktops and mobile devices.
- Educational Tool – Learn external ballistics and projectile physics.
- Free & Accessible – No downloads or registration required.
- Formula-Based Accuracy – Uses BC = m / (Cd × A).

sections.heading: Use Cases
sections.content: Who can benefit from this tool?

subSections.heading: Applications
subSections.content:
- Physics Students & Teachers – Learn projectile motion and drag effects.
- Hobbyists & DIY Projects – Analyze model rockets and projectiles.
- Engineers & Designers – Estimate aerodynamic efficiency quickly.
- Projectile Comparison – Compare BC of different projectiles for performance.
- Educational Demonstrations – Teach external ballistics concepts in class.

sections.heading: Example Calculations
sections.content: Here are some examples of how the calculation works:

subSections.heading: Example 1 – Small Projectile
subSections.content:
Mass: 0.02 kg
Drag Coefficient: 0.45
Cross-Sectional Area: 0.001 m²
Ballistic Coefficient: 44.44

subSections.heading: Example 2 – Medium Projectile
subSections.content:
Mass: 0.05 kg
Drag Coefficient: 0.35
Cross-Sectional Area: 0.002 m²
Ballistic Coefficient: 71.43
